scenario_title: Delete an absent RR versus absent RRset after prerequisites pass
test_rationale: This test isolates the underspecified deletion semantics in RFC 2136 by making the UPDATE otherwise valid and authorized under an unsigned, in-zone dynamic update, then requesting deletion of a TXT RRset that is known to be absent. Because the prerequisites only establish that the zone and queried name exist/are absent as needed, the only interesting choice is whether the server returns success for a no-op delete or emits a semantic failure such as NXRRSET. The follow-up query verifies that the targeted RRset remains absent regardless of the reply.

group_1.behavior: Treats delete_rrset targeting an absent RRset as a successful no-op after prerequisites pass.

group_2.behavior: Rejects the UPDATE as semantically invalid when the targeted RRset is not present.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 clearly defines prerequisite failures, but §3.4.2.7/§3.4.2.8 do not explicitly state whether deleting a nonexistent RR or RRset after prerequisites have already succeeded must be a no-op or an error. Servers may therefore differ on the UPDATE reply RCODE while leaving zone state unchanged.
